Crap. Crap. Crap. Crap........CRRRAAAAPPPP!

DO NOT BUY ANY BALMER WATCH EVER! I did not purchase this watch off Amazon, but did receive one through a subscription I had called, Watch Gang, it was just The Phantom, not Phantom 2. When I first received the watch I was VERY impressed by the aesthetics of the watch. Rose gold with a black face and a chocolate brown leather band and matching rose gold clasp, beautiful. However, that's the ONLY positive thing I have to say about this watch and company. The b.s. story Balmer post on their website about "Swiss Movement," "Swiss Inspired," is a GIANT LOAD. They're NOT Swiss movement at all, the thing barely keeps time losing anywhere from 1 to 3 minutes a day and sometimes more. Also, the moonphase on this watch might as well have been left on the cutting room floor, because you CANNOT reset it and its stuck on whatever time it thinks is right. The date is NEVER right after the day changes, it regularly skips at least 2 days....seriously? The mechanics are a joke, you can literally hear them struggling to move as you try to change the time/date/month. Oh and my FAVORITE part is that for whatever reason the watch thinks 9:00 pm is midnight and changes the date at about 9:00 pm EVERY SINGLE DAY! What the hell kind of crap is that Balmer? I think you all should be sued for slandering the Swiss name all over your website as you drag their name through the mud and the amount of disrespect it shows that you can sell this crap for unreasonable prices and pretend that it is quality.

Seriously people DO NOT EVER BUY ONE OF THESE WATCHES! If you just want a watch for looks then be my guest because I promise you'll be looking at your phone for the correct time and not this piece of junk. One last thing, their pricing is must have been done by about 11 different people from around the world who only own a telegraph, because their pricing ranges from about $125 up to $3000........if anyone reads this and has actually paid the MSRP they ask, please email me immediately because you clearly have money to blow and I'll take some of it and put it to good use.

Hopefully they'll go bankrupt soon and get this crap out of the watch world.

top_profane.text:
If I have a wallet with any give or extra pockets or anywhere to shove crap I collect throughout the day, I will. I end up with a 4" thick wallet full of garbage and the occasional dollar bill and standard 3 credit cards and ID.

I looked at all the super fancy (and expensive) band-type wallets and bought this because it was 12 bucks. I figured, if it sucks, I'm out 12 bucks and I'll write a review to reflect my dissatisfaction. If it lasts for 6 months, it's worth it.

Well, it's worth it. I'm buying a new one today because I bent it trying to get the clip to clip better (don't buy this for a money clip. As other reviews here point out - it does not hold form even after one time. I don't ever have cash, so I don't care about it. I put my business cards in the clip). I've shoved too many cards in it and generally beat the heck out of it and it still works well...just that bend I put in it is pinching the cards and marking up one of them. My fault.

What I WAS concerned about was the plastic lever that ejects the cards wearing out or breaking, and whether the thing would actually hold the cards in place. Plastic lever works perfect every time, and no cards fall out under regular use. If I hold it upside down and shake the crap out of it a hundred times, I might get some slippage. Otherwise, It has not been an issue.

I am a minimalist. I wear solid colored shirts and jeans and don't care if my wallet has a knife in it, or carbon fiber jazzy garbage, and I damn sure don't want any "optional leather sleeves with extra pockets" to fill up with the hundreds of receipts I get daily. I just want a thin wallet to hold my essential crap that won't make my back hurt from sitting on it while I'm driving.

That's what this is. I can't feel it, it works just fine (except the clip as mentioned), and trust me- nobody gives a crap about how cool your wallet looks. What matters to me is easy access and nothing but what I need in it.

If you want to change your Costanz-esque wallet pack-rat ways like me, and not feel like you've overspent on a hipster wallet that comes with tools and bands and a bunch of other garbage you don't need - then buy this one. For God's sake it's $12!
top_profane.title: Love It

top_profane.text: They look real. The slipper is sweaty inside and slick, not absorbent or soft. It is unlike any other UGG boot or slipper I own, and I have 20 pairs of them. The interior fur feels like a fake cheap stuffed animal. Within the first 10 minutes of wearing them, my foot was slippery and the fur was mashed down FLAT everywhere except in the arch where it was bunched up uncomfortably. They were made in VietNam. I have done the research and have identified that all the signs are there for these to be the real thing, but if this is the case, then I am done with UGGS. A total, complete POS. There is no arch support at all. The bottoms are too rigid, and thin rubbery-plastic without much give to them. They have the correct sun logo on the bottom, with the correct wrappings, box, registered trademark, so I don't know what to tell you. I got a pair of Dearfoam Firesides immediately after throwing the UGGs in the back of the closet. The Firesides are $$ cheaper, better quality, real sheepskin, made in China, like all cheap crap is, but although they are crappy, they arent as crappy as the UGGs. I have given up on finding anything of any quality anywhere online. I dont believe anything is real, the descriptions are inaccurate, if the products arrive at all then they are crap, or if they seem okay, they last about a week. I order cotton shirts, they are not cotton. I have to pay restocking fees, they have to be unworn, so you cant return stuff after it falls apart in the wash. I dont have the time or energy to keep packaging, boxes, and keep researching and arguing and paying yet more to return cheap crap items. The sellers count on that, too. Unfortunately, too many people will put up with this, like me, so the brick and mortar stores have no inventory. It will all catch up with us someday and order may be restored. But I doubt it.
top_profane.title: Either fake or UGGS has lost quality. Not sheepskin. Fake fur. Hard soles. No support. CHEAP.

THAT JUST DOESN'T MAKE ANY SENSE. SO, YOU'VE GOTTA BE PRETTY CAREFUL & PAY ATTENTION WHEN BUYING JEWELRY.

ALL OF THE "TWINKLES" OF COLORS, HOW IT "GLISTENS" IN THE LIGHT, THAT COMES FROM IT, JUST "NATURALLY" AND I SAY IT HONESTLY... BECAUSE OF IT'S SPARKLE, THE TRULY SHIMMERING COLOR OF THE STONE- IT IS JUST AMAZINGLY GORGEOUS!! IT SHOCKED ME. I DON'T THINK THIS CLASSIFIES AS "COSTUME" JEWELRY, EITHER. THIS NECKLACE LOOKS SOOOO MUCH MORE EXPENSIVE THAN IT IS.

YOU'LL WANT TO KNOW MY EXPERIENCE WITH IT, SO FAR. IT'S WORTH TAKING THE TIME TO TELL YOU. IT'S IMPRESSED ME... AND MANY OTHER PEOPLE. WHICH IS WHY IT HAS ACTUALLY SHOCKED ME. SO, I THINK IT TRULY DESERVES AN HONEST REVIEW. I HAVE BOUGHT A TON OF STUFF... LITERALLY THOUSANDS OF DOLLARS (ACTUALLY THE TRUTH), FROM AMAZON SO FAR. BUT, I HARDLY EVER BUY JEWELRY ONLINE. I got the BLUE stone necklace and it constantly changes shades in different lighting-- and, when you're looking right at it, not even moving. It's really hard to describe... BUT. THIS IS A GOOD THING.
*** FUNNY THING.... EVEN MY CAT HAS NOTICED THIS NECKLACE! WHEN I SAY THAT IT SPARKLES, SHIMMERS & HAS FLASHES OF COLOR COMING FROM IT... I'M NOT KIDDING. SO MUCH SO, THAT MY CAT HAS NOTICED IT & TRIES TO PLAY WITH IT, WHEN I WEAR IT. I MADE THE MISTAKE OF SLEEPING WITH IT ON... AND, SHE "CAUGHT" IT, BUT, WOKE ME UP AND ACCIDENTALLY SCRATCHED ME. EVEN MY CAT WAS "DRAWN" TO IT'S FLASHES OF COLOR, THE SHIMMERING THAT COMES OUT OF THE STONE. I HAD SCRATCHES FROM HER TRYING TO PLAY WITH SOMETHING SHE THOUGHT WAS "MOVING". THAT, IN ITSELF, SHOULD HELP EXPLAIN HOW MUCH SPARKLE AND COLOR "MOVEMENT" COMES OUT OF THIS STONE. -- TRUE STORY.
IT'S NOT JUST "BLUE". IT HAS EVERY SHADE OF BLUE IMAGINABLE-- AND, THEN EVEN MORE. THIS STONE TRULY SPARKLES, IT SHIMMERS, IT GLITTERS.

Leading me to actually buy more pieces. After seeing it, wearing it and it holding up thru everything... I know that the jewelry is worth buying. AFTER ALL THE COMPLIMENTS... THE STONE IS MESMERIZING!! I usually don't like bold necklaces... and this one really isn't... but, if they have even bigger stones on their necklaces- I would wear it, in a heartbeat! Especially after the experience I've had with it so far, in the short time that I've owned it. The thing is... I DON'T THINK YOU CAN TRULY CAPTURE THE TRUE NATURAL SHIMMER, THE SHINE, THE SPARKLE & JUST THE EXTREME BEAUTY OF THIS PIECE, UNLESS YOU'RE LOOKING RIGHT AT IT WITH YOIR OWN EYES. IT'S NEARLY IMPOSSIBLE NOT TO NOTICE THIS NECKLACE... AND, IF YOU'RE IN THE SUN... YOU'LL NEED TO BE WEARING SUNGLASSES. THE FLASHES ARE BRIGHT... I'M NOT KIDDING. DON'T HANG THIS NECKLACE ON YOUR REAR-VIEW MIRROR, WHILE YOUR DRIVING, EITHER. I HAVE A CRYSTAL ORNAMENT HANGING IN MY CAR & IF THE SUN CATCHES IT JUST RIGHT, IT'LL SHINE STRAIGHT INTO MY EYE. THIS NECKLACE IS A LOT MORE SHINIER AND HAS MORE "BRILLIANCE"TO IT-- SO, IT COULD BE DANGEROUS, I'M SURE.

THE GLIMMER, SHINING & FLASHES WHEN YOU MOVE... AND, WHEN THE COLOR CHANGES IN DIFFERENT LIGHTING. If you like BLING, you'll LOVE this necklace. But, however... if you like an understated, simply beautiful necklace.... you'll LOVE this one, too. IT'S TRULY WORTH EVERY PENNY AND IF YOU'RE READING THIS.... YOU'RE RESEARCHING IT TO SEE IF IT'S WORTH BUYING. I PROMISE YOU... IT IS MOST DEFINITELY A SURE SHOT BUY!!
**** UNFORTUNATELY... I KNOW THAT THERE ARE PEOPLE WHO LEAVE BAD REVIEWS, TO LURE YOU AWAY FOR SOME REASON, OR, TO GET YOU TO BUY SOMETHING ELSE... AND, I KNOW THAT SOME PEOPLE ACTUALLY GET PAID TO LEAVE BAD REVIEWS... LEADING US TO NOT BE ABLE TO TRUST MOST OF THEM, IF NOT ANY, OF THE REVIEWS THAT SOME PEOPLE LEAVE. IT'S HARD TO DESCRIBE... BUT, I TRIED. I CAN ASSURE YOU THAT IF YOU LOVE "BLING", THEN, YOU'LL LOVE THIS NECKLACE. BUT, IT'S ALSO AN "UNDERSTATED", REALLY SHINY PIECE THAT GOES WITH ALMOST ANYTHING. IT'S BEEN A GREAT BUY FOR ME... HAS TURNED INTO ONE OF MY FAVORITE PIECES, WITHOUT QUESTION. I TRULY LOVE THIS NECKLACE..IT WILL BE A GREAT GIFT FOR ANYONE!!
I HOPE THIS HELPS SOMEONE'S DECISION A LITTLE EASIER. 🙂

top_rant.text:
Wow! I am soooo not sure if there'll be enough space on this review page!

First off, if you need a Steampunk look or are thinking of modding your persona to anything of the "likeness"... GET THESE NOW!
--------------------------------------
And then continue reading this review!
If you're seeking any of the following...; then you need to buy these!
1. The Most Professional, Ultra Quality Sunglasses...that money can buy! (Seriously!... I'm going to be 38 this year, and I've never been more satisfied and bafflingly impressed with a sunglasses purchase, in my entire life!... Until now!!!!!)
EVERY ASPECT of EVERYTHING INCLUDED with THIS PRODUCT, should BEYOND MEET your EXPECTATIONS!!!! (I will gladly spend every character possibly to elaborate on this!)
Get your cup of coffee, your smoke, or your focus pill, and take a 3 minute break and then come back to read, You're going to need it!

I'll start with the small stuff and build my way back!
The packaging it was mailed in, could've been more protected. I wasn't impressed with it at all! Plus the Penske guy who delivered it, was rude and didn't even knock! He pulled up, ran to the porch... Tossed the package onto my porch, then ran back and sped off!

Aside from that... The awesomeness began!

The box: White with "Rocknight" Logo Embossed, with quality construction! Definitely not some cheap print job! Great protective cardboard material... Something you'd be proud you receive as a Gift.... And even prouder to give as a Gift! Just the thought of watching my kids or friends tearing through beautiful paper, to reveal their much deserved and needed Gift!... It would put any other gift reveal to shame!
The box is also properly constructed! I'll admit, slightly hard to actually open the flaps that enclosed the actually beauty within! But it wasn't impossible

After opening the box, out came the Elegance of the hard case, that is not cheaply made! It's designed with the "Rough Worker" & "Serious Professional" in mind! It...IS...rather bulky; but displays and produces the greatest "resilience like" protection and quality that you can get for your money! The outer netting of the case gives added POM (Peace of Mind) to the idea that the case; when not attached to your person, or clipped too your personal effects... Will not slip or slide out of your hands or off of anything that it's set on (short of an object with a residing angle of greater than 45° Diagonally). The plastic clip that is affixed to the case, is sturdy and solidly attached to the case! The zipper style outer edge of the case makes way for easy, quiet, and sleek opening/closing and securing of the case. It also has a "Rocknight" label brandished and securely sewn in next to the zipper..... This is no, cheap case! And it's also "Standard/Deluxe... Jean, Formal, Coat, Trench.. Pocket Sized! A Tad Bulky (3"), but "POM Bulky"!

Upon unzipping the case and opening it, you'll discover everything securely wrapped and packed in protective film, some of which is easy to unwrap or unroll, but secure. Others are a bit more "securely" wrapped in either thicker plastic, or hardened "Id Tag" style plastic!
It's all wrapped and protected..Elegantly!

There's a Maintenance and Informant excerpt, printed on top quality paper with dark but legible ink. It's neatly folded and compressed into a tiny ID Tag type of plastic. It's basically a "care card", informing you of any and all limitations of the glasses. The ID Tag is included to deliver the cleaning and care instructions, but doubles as a protective carrying case for the included and impressively constructed "cleaning cloth"!

There's a dual Philips/Flat head portable screwdriver keychain included for "on the go" tune up work.

A thickly wrapped "protective cleaning cloth" and alike material style "soft carrying case" that each brandish the "Rocknight" logo in in "heat applied embossing", with clear instructions as to why it should be used to clean the glasses only, instead of typical cloths and handkerchiefs..'which easily leave scratches' as mentioned above, the "cleaning cloth" has a thick plastic ID Tag style carrying case that easily can attach to the included screwdriver set keychain, or can simply be placed in the hard case for elegant and casually convenient cleaning and polishing of the sunglasses!

The soft carrying case is "draw string bag" style; utilizing the same type of material as the included cleaning/polishing cloth, and uses a Sturdy Polycord with thick tired ends, to tie the bag closed, but also easily opens to allow super fast retrieval of the glasses if needed, complete with the brandished Heat Applied "Rocknight" logo Embossed on the bag ! The bag itself was designed to automatically polish the mirrored/highly reflective outside of the polarized lenses Everytime you place the sunglasses in, or remove them for use!
The glasses, soft case, polishing cloth, and screwdriver set are all able to be Elegantly, Safely, Properly, & Compactively stored together, within the hard case... For short or long distance travel!

It also comes with a "Test Tag", complete with QR Code & Polarized "Rocknight" Logo Sticker!
It's included so that once you put the glasses on for the first time, (or if you're showcasing them) you can immediately see the true polarization of the lenses!
The sticker appears as a "Dark Grey" & Rectangular. And has the faintest image of the "Rocknight" logo embedded within. It's not actually viewable to the naked eye at first, but is pretty obvious that there's something special about it due to the rounded rectangular tag that it's applied on with the QR Code sitting boldly to the left of it on the same tag. If closely inspected in proper light (at a slight angle), the logo can be seen, but only as "embedded".
Once you put the glasses on and look at the tag... The Rocknight logo comes to life! The polarized lenses give it an instant "3D" look that shows it as ~ A darkened logo surrounded by a lighter background; in the right lense (if you close one eye and look, then switch eyes in the same manner) And a lighter logo surrounded by a darkened background, in the left lense!
With both eyes open... A "strobe" effect of light and dark flickering, "3D" style!

Now onto the "Crème De La Crème"; The Sunglasses!
The construction is of superior craftsmanship...I'd know!
My 9 month old took them and attempting to pull the ear stems apart! And after stretching them out pretty far... (I thought he actually broke them or at least bent them out of contouritable ability, to a point of no return...permanent damage! But nope!) They sprung right back into proper shape!
The lenses have lovely quarter inch wide alloyed awnings that extend across the top of the lenses, to block out unwanted sunlight, and also have the same awnings on the sides!
They have spring assisted ear stems, that keep the proper tension at all times to ensure secure opening and closing of the stems.
The architecture and design of the frames beyond the ear stems is of superb craftsmanship and rocks the traditional "Steampunk" look!
The lenses (Depending on which style you go with...) are of quality construction and provide perfect protection and darkened tint on the inside for optimum viewing premiums! And have a flawless mirrored finish on the outer side of the lenses!... This is not your; Typical, cheap, oil~based, smudgey, knockoff, temporary, half~assed, "made in China"... "Mirrored~Finish"!
This is a True Mirrored Finish!!! And "if" you so happen to get smudges and natural body oils or dandruff on the inside or outside of the polarized lenses, the polishing cloth... WORKS!!! Twice I've had to polish the lenses in 2 days of constant wear. And not only did the cloth clean and polish the lenses... But it restored them to mint reflection! Like perfect mirrored glass!
Looked like I'd just taken them out of the plastic for the first time again!

So. To wrap this review up and hope it all fits, and I don't have to edit it down any...
I told you it'd be worth reading!
Now go and get your second cup of coffee... But first, ADD to cart!!!!, if...your either seeking the best damn pair of sunglasses you're going to buy ever, either for yourself...a friend...Or a loved one!
For the price you pay (and everything that's included and no where near cheaply made!), they could've charged $99-$120 a pair!
This is Steampunk Gear! The plastic decor goggles... Aren't even this cheaply priced! And they don't do a damn thing!
These glasses work! They are of top quality! They come with everything needed (So no quick runs to Wal-Mart or the dollar tree when the mail arrives...for protective supplies!)...And the accessories that are included aren't cheaply made either!

So, select the colour lense and frame you want... And lock your purchase in!
Unless your getting Silver & Silver!... Don't touch those, you don't want them... Only I do!

I'm pretty sure you enjoyed reading this, if you got this far!

I enjoyed writing it...

And wow... It all fit! (Just like this does in your budget, and everything that's included does in the case!)

Just remember, leave the Silver & Silver option alone... (still mine!)

top_rant.text:
Okay first things first. I LOVE these shoes! Let me explain why:

1.) They're sexy without a doubt
2.) Very comfortable
3.) Beautifully designed
4.) Internal Foot Padding
5.) Platform Helps With High Heel

Please let me explain. I have rheumatoid arthritis, needless to say, I have VERY weak ankles. My doctor once told me my ankles looked as if they would collapse. Being that I have this disability, heels have always been my desire, but never been possible for me. My mother always scolded me for even trying to wear heels when I knew I couldn't. I wouldn't last 20 minutes in heels, not to mention HIGH heels. I've always had to wear wedges instead and even majority of them were a "no-go" for me because of the height. My ankles would burn in them. However, I purchased these shoes after reading all of the great reviews on them and I'm very happy I did!

These are the FIRST pair of pumps (not second...not third...but FIRST) pair of pumps that I am able to so much as STAND in let alone walk in like I own them. *snaps finger* The fact that they are "chunky" or thick pumps helps out with balance ALOT. Not to mention the tall platform attached to the in-step. They feel like 2" heels like another reviewer stated. I'm very excited to have them because like I said, I would see women prance around in their 5+ inch heeled stilettos w/ or w/ out a platform like they don't hurt! I couldn't wrap my brain around it. I've always been slightly self-conscious and fairly insecure and ashamed of my disability because I like to be as feminine as possible. I'm a girly girl and i'll admit it. Every woman wants to feel sexy, like a hot mama at least one day in her life. Heels complete that look. So without me being able to sport any, I've always felt a bit ashamed as an 18 year old college student. BUT THESE....they don't feel like tall heels at all!

Now here are some things I want to let you in on...

1.) The Size: I know people said they run pretty big, but keep in mind that these are sold in only Medium sizes. With that being said, all you narrow or wide footers need to improvise. I, myself, am a wide foot. I ALWAYS have the hardest time sizing my feet because they are VERY VERY SMALL but WIDE. I know it can be frustrating. I can still fit a size 5 in flat shoes but heels are trickier because of the curves the shoe has. Since I am a wide foot, I ordered a 7.5 first...then I thought to myself "these are Medium sized shoes, I'd rather be safe than sorry". With little to no heel shopping experience, I found myself very skeptical to order heels online. So that "better safe than sorry" instinct kicked in. I canceled that order immediately and ordered an 8. Remember that thing I said about wide footers? Well, it worked! I'm glad I ordered an 8! They fit me perfectly with just enough comfort room!
LADIES...you have to remember that EVERYONE'S feet are different whether they're long / short, wide / medium / narrow, chubby / slender. Not to mention everyone's HEEL size will be different from their SNEAKER size. The construction of the shoes are different. SO YOU CANNOT ADVISE EVERYONE TO GO A HALF SIZE DOWN!!!! Buyers, you have to buy for yourself and shop smart. If you know you have wide but short feet, buy for the width, you can ALWAYS make shoes that are too big, smaller with inserts and insoles but you CAN NEVER make shoes that are too small, bigger. (shoe stretches aren't worth it majority of the time). I always say, I'd rather have too much room than not enough. The 8 fit my feet JUST right. ALWAYS buy shoes for your width, the length can always be readjusted. Remember, these are MEDIUM fitting shoes, NOT wide, NOT narrow. YOU have to improvise. I ordered a half size bigger to accommodate my wide width. Narrow footers, order a half size smaller to accommodate your narrow feet. My sister has medium width but LONG feet. She tried the shoes on as well and she fits them PERFECTLY as well. See how it works? She wears an 8 in medium shoes. She wouldn't have needed to improvise for her shoe size like I had to. Now we're both happy! HAHA.

2.) The Comfort Level: When I first put the shoes on, I said "oh yeah, these feel nice". I stood up and walked along the carpet of the dorm room I stay in and felt amazing in them!! They are unbelievably comfortable! I was shocked that they had comfortable padding inside of them. Breckelle's brand of shoes like these were ROCK HARD. Here's the only drawback to walking in these shoes all night, they LACK padding down by the ball of your foot. Everywhere else has nice padding except here. So be prepared to purchase some insertable insole paddings for extra support.

3.) Like many people said before THEY ARE SLIPPERY on the bottoms. I stay in a dorm room with half carpet, half hard rubber floors. So with that being said, you cannot feel the slipperiness on these types of floors. However, I tested them out by taking one of the heels and running the bottom across our wooden bathroom door (in the illusion of hardwood flooring). Would you believe me if I told you the shoe almost slipped right out of my hand?? These are dangerously slippery on hard, slick flooring!!! YOU WILL INJURE YOURSELF IN THESE SHOES. They are a death trap. So like one reviewer said and I completely agree, YOU WILL NEED SOME GRIT TAPE FOR THESE SHOES! You will need some anti-slip tape or something!! These shoes are TOO SEXY to be busting your stuff on the ground in them, especially if you're wearing a dress for the evening. (ugh talk about a ruined night / date). I fault them for making a defective bottom for these shoes because believe me, YOU WILL SLIP. They have NO grip whatsoever. So definitely, before you wear them outside of your home (or on your hard and even flooring) PLEASE buy grit tape and stick it to the bottom of these shoes!!

4.) The Material: I ordered these in size 8 Black - New or something like that. They're the all black suede kind. The heel is even black on them. Now, if you ordered ANY kind of suede you know they're prone to staining right? Especially the light colored suede colors. Anybody here who's owned Timberland boots knows the struggle of keeping suede looking like new! I love the way they look and feel! They're so sexy looking! My comment was "you'd get laid wearing those" lol and my sister said "you'd get laid WHILE wearing those!" they're a man magnet (at least they seem like they would be). But I came up with the conclusion that you shouldn't wear these suede boots in the winter time. Suede is more prone to damage in the winter because of the snow on the ground or the salt on the ground. They tend to get wet, lose dye, or get white stains on them in the winter. So if you aren't planning on being indoors at a concert or party in these, I wouldn't wear them in the winter. They're sexy for parties and concerts but not for casual wear outdoors in the winter. Well, I ended up buying TWO more pairs of these shoes (now you understand my obsession) tonight. I brought the tan leather and the black leather ones. These will do JUST FINE in the upcoming winter. The others will not. Be cautious of that! Next summer, I'll purchase the Burgundy and Royal Blue suede boots, but not this season.

5.) They DO NOT have a zipper. Be prepared to sit down somewhere and unlace and lace up these boots every time you want to put them on or take them off.

Anyway, they're AWESOME shoes! They're my first set of pumped heels that's not a wedge! So I can't express my excitement enough!! Just take my little advice I put above and you'll love these shoes as well!!! They're amazing!!! Remember, be prepared to purchase some grit tape for the slippery bottoms and some foot insole cushions for the balls of your feet (your front right next to your toes) because the boots have comfortable padding everywhere else besides there. You'll begin to notice that hard spot after a few hours. So take note of that! They're tall heels, but they have a tall platform to accommodate you. So beginners don't feel bad, THESE ARE FOR YOU! Just subtract the platform height (2") from the heel height (5") which is 5-2=3" so believe it or not, you're literally only handling 3" heels!! You can do it!! They're so comfy! I just brought two more pair tonight!!!

BUY!!! BUY!!! BUY!!!!

top_rant.text:
Ok girls, face it no bra is a 5 star rating just for the fact that no one wants to wear a bra!!! But if you have to wear one, then this is a 5 star review bra. I would never have thought I'd put pictures up, and I still hesitate in doing so, but I really feel it's important because I know I cant be the only one having so many problems finding a good fitting bra!!!! I spent hours and hours looking for a white, no wire, seamless bra in my size, and to find one in my price range of under $50. This was the only one I found that I knew and trusted. It also takes hours because I really do look at other reviewers. I start by one star reviews and work my way up. I also look at who the reviewers are, for instance, one bra I looked at had a lot of great reviews but when I looked at the reviews, they were really off grammatically. One was a guy who reviewed his G Cup bra (I know there can be transgender reviewers, but not usually ones who said they bought a G Cup bra). I see many Chinese companies who, if they don't have good reviews on a product, will put new listings up and you will see maybe 5 or 6 reviews and they are almost all the same.

At this size I've only worn Bali or Glamorise so I've always liked Glamorise and was surprised to see the negative reviews. Many reviewers said that the band seemed too small. One thing I wonder is if they are measuring correctly according to how Glamorise says to do it. Everytime I've gone to a website to order a bra, they always say to measure under the bust (around the rib cage) for the first measurement and that's the band size. But on this product page it said to measure above the bust to get the band measurement, which gives a bigger size than measuring under. For other bras I can measure a 46 to 48 G and for this bra I measured a 50 G - big difference when you're buying a bra! I could have also gone with a 52 G since I always end up hooking it in the first set of hooks when there are usually 3-4 sets of hooks.

I ordered the 50 G and it said it was going to be delivered on Easter Sunday. I thought ya, but on Sunday it will say the delivery will change to the next day (I have prime, but sometimes the post office delays the delivery). I'm alone - no husband - no kids, so I was sitting in my kitchen on Easter excitedly waiting for my package to arrive like it's Christmas day!!!! As the day went on I thought "yup it's not going to come today". Then a few minutes later I see the mail truck. I live in a multi family apartment house and they usually leave packages on the porch. But then I hard a noise outside my door, so I went and opened it and it was the mail lady! I think I surprised her! She hands me the package and I'm almost breathless as I thank her, "thank you for delivery on Sunday and thank you for delivering on Easter!", she looks at me weirdly as I slam the door I her face to run back in with my new present! .

Many people don't realize what a challenge it can't be for large busted women to put a bra on. My mom taught me (some 43 or so years ago) to put your arms through the straps first then reach behind to hook it. It wasn't until a few years ago that I talked to woman who looked at me weird and said "you're supposed to hook it in the front first than twist it around to the back" - like duh! LOL!!! Say what? So back then I tried their method and the twisting it around to the back almost gave me bra burn, kind of like rug burn!

So here starts the acrobatic show of a large busted woman putting on her new bra. I'm in the bathroom and try to put it on my way. I put my arms through the straps and reach behind me to hook it. Every time I got the hook close to the eye, one of the straps would pop out of my hand. After a few attempts, and getting out of breath, I figure it's time to try the "other way". So I hook the bra in the front under my boobs, and I start twisting it to the back, thinking to myself that I still really don't like this skin burning method!! Once around to the back, and again out of breath, I attempt to put my left arm through the strap to bring it up to my shoulder. Notice I say attempt. I can get my hand through but that's it, I will never get my arm through it. This is when my Christmas gift high crashed to the ground. As I'm about to give up, I think to myself that if they said to measure around the top of your bust to get the band size, then maybe I should have hooked it over the top my boobs before twisting it to the back. So I twist it back to the front, again getting bra burn, to unhook it and rehook it over the top of my boobs. I then twist to the back again, getting less bra burn this time which makes me hopeful, then put my left hand through the strap and to my again growing excitement....... I can get my whole arm in there, albeit snuggly, and I'm able to get the strap onto my shoulder!!!!!!! I then confidently get my right arm in and up goes that strap!! This is not only a sports bra but also a minimizing bra - double firmness!! That's great but I realize now, with my bra hooked over my boobs, that I now need to get the girls into the cups! I'm now cursing the MAN that invented bras!!! I wonder how he would feel stuffing his testicles into a testicle holder made of panty hose material (which I believe man created pantyhose too)!!! So I cram them in and adjust them, because if your 56 like me and have been large busted your whole life, well you know that things aren't always where they used to be!!!

Once I get the girls adjusted, then the straps adjusted, I finally turn to look in the full length mirror and the skies part - the sun comes shinning out - and the angels sing!!! I see a bra that fits - no, it really fits!!! My girls are up - in fact they feel like they are standing at attention saluting back at me!!!, I don't have uni-boob, and as I turn I don't see side boob - that never happens!! When I completely turn around I see a slight bit of back boob, but face it when your overweight is not going to be smooth - it's not a miracle bra. I then put on the white, thin, summer shirt that made me start the search for this bra, because the only bra I had that fits is beige with lace and you could see the whole thing through the shirt. So, I put the shirt on and once again turn to look in the full length mirror and BAM look at that!!!! Oh happy day!!!! I look like a normal woman wearing a good fitting bra, and wearing a cute summer tank shirt!

One other thing, you have to understand that if you wear a 50 G bra you cannot expect that the straps won't tire out a bit at some point during the day from holding that weight up, and you have to do the familiar act of reaching around back to pull the band down while pulling the girls up - the bra, after all, is not made of steal LOL! I will now be purchasing another! I hope this helps someone out in getting a good fit!

top_rant.text:
Pleated cheer skirts seem to have fallen out of favor and These box-pleated cheer skirts with the contrast-color insets appear to be unpopular if not particularly hard to find. The cheerleading supply houses tend to have a minimum order & the online secondhand marketplaces are a crap shoot so I was lucky to find one at a reasonable price in the correct color-combo (Columbia blue & white) to match an 80s cheer sweater I got for a screaming deal on eBay. The more pleats, the splashier they look; This one has 8 pleats- the bare minimum for a legit cheerleader look & the minimum number of pleats in this type of skirt. 16 is the maximum but I think its overkill. 10 or 12 pleats is optimal, IMO but you take what you can get. My waist measures 30.5” & my hips are 42”, I’m 5’5” & my dress size is a 10 or 12- which isn’t great because for Letter-size garments a 10 is paired with an 8(which is too small) and a 12 is paired to a 14(which is too large. So, naturally I fell between sizes for this too. I ordered an extra-large but I should have ordered a large. That was the sizing recommendation Amazon gave me but historically, those haven’t been very reliable- especially with shoes & when shopping for men’s/unisex tops for myself. Yeesh. You try to buy a simple teeshirt that isn’t a clingy cap-sleeve “girly” tee and they steer me to an XL!! Is it any wonder I have a complex, Amazon?! So, if it’s not a huge pain in the tukus, I’ll be sending this one back and exchanging it for a large. If that’s even possible, that is! Most websites that sell cheerleading uniforms have a “no return” policy; even stock items like this skirt (which I’ve seen elsewhere online including the manufacturer, Augusta Sportswear) are final sale. So fingers crossed because if I can’t send it back, I either have to deal with it or try to sell it because with the way this skirt is constructed, taking it in or tightening the elastic is not an option. This seems like a reasonably well-constructed garment but I’m not 100% thrilled with it. The skirt & sweater are the same color-combo: columbia blue & white but they were made by two entirely different companies (2-3 decades apart, no less) so it’s not a perfect color match; it’s close enough though, so I’m not stressing, let alone docking points for that. Ditto the temporary stay-stitching on the hem. Well, not for it being there. It doesn’t confuse or anger me. I’m not an idiot & That was true BEFORE I went to design school or even when I started to learn to sew (age 11,12ish)I know it’s only there to keep the pleats in position & meant to be removed- UNLIKE some people I won’t mention whose review was undeservingly voted as helpful (despite being so misinformed that it amounts to a string of lies) presumably by people who are similarly situated in the cognition department. I am knocking off half a star because I DON’T like the way it was done. I can almost (but not quite!) forgive the gal who sent hers back in a huff & gave it that stunningly incorrect 1 star review. Or at any rate, I can see how she might have arrived there because the stitching & thread used to temporarily tack-down the pleats is fairly indistinguishable from the actual hem. An X on each pleat or even a longer machine stitch, ideally in a totally different color or maybe some note on the packing slip would have helped them out with the folks that gave this one star and/or a snotty review. I’m pretty knowledgable about garment construction so I can only imagine how confusing it would be to someone who’s only experience with clothing is buying it or how big a pain it might be for a novice to remove without hacking at the actual hem. I DO know what I’m doing and I still had to give it a second look. I’m lucky that I decided to return this one because If I’d kept it, there’s a good chance I’d have started hacking away at the hem! I think as long as I’m careful to only snip the threads at the juncture of the pleats in the seam going over the tops of them, use a seam ripper judiciously & pull at the thread on the back, it should go fast. Since I intend to exchange this one(fingers crossed!) for the correct size, I haven’t removed the stitching & therefore cannot speak to the amount of flare to the skirt. Again- with only 8 pleats it’s bound to be fairly modest. My main issues are with construction & the care instructions. I’m not that thrilled with the elastic waist but I can see the benefit so I’ll let it slide- especially when/if I can exchange for one that isn’t too large. These types of pleated skirts always look sharper if there is top-stitching on the edges of the pleats & between them (between the waistband & top of the pleat) to keep the tops of the inset piece in place. There should also be some tacking, bar-stitching, backstitching or something at the tops of the pleats so they don’t come unstitched- as at least one of the pleats on this one was when I pulled it out of the bag. That’s how the old ones were made. Those could also cost twice this much 25, 30 years ago so one needs to adjust expectations. Additionally, a couple of the pleats seem to be slightly uneven in length. I’m planning on correcting that oversight of workmanship to the edges of the pleats. Top-stitching on a knitted fabric is nerve-wracking enough without having to hand tack the tops of the pleats on a brand new skirt to keep them from coming loose before I can even wear the thing so l’m knocking off half a star, which I think is generous considering that I’ll have to sew by machine & By hand. I’m not a fan of The fabric; it doesn’t have the same crisp look as old cheer skirts made of polyester double knit or even gabardine(which is more common for a tennis skirt). It’s close to double-knit or perhaps it’s what passes for it nowadays but it’s flimsier & seems slightly stretchier. There’s no real body to it so I’m knocking off half a star for the fabric. I’m hoping that I can give it some more body with sizing or starch but obviously I can’t make that gamble until I have the correct size. And a gamble it will be. Which brings me to my biggest gripe. When I pulled this out of the envelope, I was no more surprised to see wrinkles than I was by the temporary stitching at the hem. What DID take me by surprise however- besides the tiny snag in the fabric, that is- was after I pulled out the iron & ironing board and- out of force of habit more than anything else- looked at the care label and saw “Do Not Iron, Press or steam”?!?!? Are you freaking kidding me?!? It’s bad enough that this skirt is too big (which is on me) lacks any sharpness to the pleats or body to the fabric; How exactly do you suggest I get these infernal friggin’ creases out?!? There’s one in particular that is SO DEEP that I initially took it for a flaw- or should I say, another flaw- in the fabric. If I can’t get it out, then doesn’t that MAKE it a flaw? I was going to give it a quick press with some spray sizing to crisp it up a bit but I was afraid to iron it on my iron’s dedicated “polyester” setting with a pressing cloth or even to give it a quick steam with my steamer. It’s not as if there’s Lycra in the fabric to justify forbidding the use of an iron. It’s not as if polyester is some delicate fiber that requires special care- like wool, silk or even RAYON- either. There’s another label- behind the one listing care instructions more restrictive than a Wall Street scumbag’s parole. It has a date on it, presumably the manufacture date: 04/01/22. April Fools’ Day?! So, Does that mean this IS a joke & they ARE freaking kidding me? Given the number of issues- even for a modestly priced skirt- & every reasonable instinct I have to remedy half of them being shot down right out of the gate, the idea that it’s a joke makes a certain amount of sense. I’m not laughing, mind you but it IS kind of funny. I’ve lost track of how many stars I’m knocking off but anything less than three and I’ll talk myself right out of ordering the correct size and what good is a cheerleading SWEATER without the skirt? Exactly!
This is meant to fit at the natural waist, not low on the waist. Unless you’re on the low end of plus size, Apple shaped or very bottom-heavy AND thick-waisted , don’t order the XL. If you’re between sizes like me, order down- especially if you’re an hourglass shape (like me) or pear shaped with a slim waist.

top_rant.text:
*Updated my review from 5 stars to 3 due to a flaw found and described at the bottom of my review.

------------------EXTERNALS----------------

SIZE/WEIGHT - it's large for a watch, however it's actually not as large as I'd thought it would be. It's just the right size (in
my opinion). If you commonly wear long sleeves or button ups, you might find the sleeve cuffs are not able to fit over the
watch. This may bother some, but a small price I'm willing to pay in order to wear the gear I want on my wrist. Ironically, this is
also a pro in my book as it ensures, the watch's solar face is never covered. The weight comes in at 3.52oz (100 grams)

APPEAL - I believe this is one of the best looking G-shocks currently on the market. It just looks like it's ready to be
put to hard work in the field, yet stylish enough to wear casually - unlike most other digitally dominated G-shocks that
have more of a "field-duty" appearance, which I didn't want personally. My first day of wearing it, I received a compliment on it, so that's a plus!

BUILD/QUALITY - It's built like a tank. It has a fair amount of weight to it, but is not overly cumbersome. It feels stout on the wrist. The build quality is fantastic. All materials used appear high-quality and feel extremely durable. Most notably, the buttons and crown. All 4 buttons and the crown are metal. It doesn't appear as if any corners were cut in it's build.

BAND - The Band is a tough resin material that certainly feels capable of withstanding a beating and very comfortable on the wrist. However the brushed metal band holder is a slight negative. It's large and doesn't hold the watch band as well as I'd like it to.

BUTTON LAYOUT AND FUNCTIONS:
The easiest way to break down navigating through the watch's features is understanding the button's primary functionalities and purpose (pic. included to help visualize)

BUTTON PRIMARY FUNCTIONS:

C = Adjusts what you prefer to show by default on your digital display. Also acts as a secondary option setting to the primary in some settings
B = This is a "master button" of sort. This navigates through the non sensor features listed below. Holding B down for 2
seconds in any mode will return you to your digital default display.
L = Back Light only
A = ABC-T functions (Altitude, Barometer, Compass, and Thermometer). This navigates through all of the watch's embedded hardware sensors listed.
CROWN = the crown has a dual purpose. 1. to set watch preferences and set time. 2. Adjust units of measurement or calibration corrections while you're in any one of the above "B" or "A" modes.

MODES FOR EACH BUTTON:

C = Adjust your default digital display readings to: digital time (World City Time, UTC, or home time) -> Moon Age -> Day and Date -> Barometer graph and date. These settings cannot changed by pulling out the crown.
B = World time mode (displays your alternative time you've set) -> Digital Tide data -> Stop Watch -> Timer -> Alarm -> R/C (Remote
Atomic Data) (pull out the crown while on any mode to make a change to that mode)
L = Back light activation
A = Barometer -> Compass -> Altitude -> and Temperature (pull out the crown while on the mode to make a change to the units of measurement or make accuracy corrections). If you last checked the compass and go back to time keeping mode, it will automatically first show the compass when A is pressed again.
CROWN = *pulling out the crown while not in a feature/mode activates the watch's settings and scrolling navigates through the various features* Home Time Setting -> Daylight Savings Time (DST) setting -> Audio Feedback on/mute -> Auto back-light on/off -> Back-light duration 1sec/3sec -> 12/24hr time reading -> P.save (Power saving mode) on/off.

*Again, pulling out the crown while you're in a mode listed in the A or B button functions will navigate to sub-settings of that mode (ex. Pressing A and going to the Barometer and then pulling out the crown will allow you to manually change the Barometric pressure reading or; while the crown is pulled out in Barometer reading and then pressing B will switch over to change the measurement of units).

-------------------------------FEATURES--------------------------------------------

TIME: You can set your time up to 29 world cities (all world time zones). While setting the time in this fashion, the second hand will act as your choice selector to select one of the abbreviated world times that's on the outer ring inside of the dial (ex.UTC, NYC, LON etc.) . This will automatically set the watch time based upon your selection. Otherwise, you can manually set the hour and minute hand to your preference.

---In addition you can set to have quick access to check 1 World City time (out of the 29 available) in addition to UTC (Universal Coordinated Time). So you'll have the ability to quickly check 3 times within a matter of seconds: Home Time, World City Time (based on whatever you selected), and UTC.

REMOTE CONTROLLED ATOMIC TIME KEEPING (R/C) - The R/C features options are "Auto" or "Off" to set your preferences if you want the watch to automatically receive a signal to sync with the atomic clock or turn it off. You can also do this manually if preferred. Once you manually or automatically receive a signal, the digital interface will read "R/C! L1/L2/L3". "R/C! *blank* or "R/C! L1" indicates no signal or a weak signal and "RC! L3" indicates a strong signal. It will then digitally store it's sync date and time so you know when it last received a signal.

---Upon unboxing the watch on the 3rd of January 2017, the watch last received it's signal in May 2016 according to the digital memory. On my first night of owning it, it automatically received a signal at 3:30 AM and synced successfully. I live in in Georgia (US) and during it's sync it maintained L2 and L3 signals while indoors in cloudy weather. So far it has received a signal daily (usually very early in the morning (1am-4am).

------Note - The watch will not receive a update or signal if the watch is in a sleep state, the crown is pulled out, during a Barometric reading, or if the countdown timer is active. Even with my Power Saving mode on, I have so far received a daily radio signal while sleeping.

DAY LIGHT SAVINGS TIME (DST) - you can turn Day Light Savings Time to "AUTO/ON/OFF" in the settings if you want the watch to automatically adjust for DST or not. "AUTO" automatically sets the time to match DST upon it changing. DST "ON/OFF" allows you to manually engage DST time.

AUDIO TONES - You can turn on or off the audio tactile feedback by navigating to "KEY" and rotating the dial to "KEY" or "MUTE". MUTE disables all audio tones while navigating through the watches various features.

BACK-LIGHT/NIGHT VISIBILITY: Back-light is easily readable at night with it's dual illumination that illuminates both the analog dial and digital interface. It can be set to automatically activate by selecting "AUTO *" to "ON or OFF". If the Auto Back light is turned on, it will automatically engage the back-light if you tilt your watch up and angled (as if you're checking the time) and you can select for it to engage for 1 sec or 3 sec. "Off" turns off this feature and you can manually engage it as needed by pressing L, whilst still offering the 1sec or 3sec illumination options.

---The manually states quite a few times in various locations throughout that the back-light is a battery hog. It doesn't provide specifics, however it seems to indicate that the light draws the most power from the battery out of all the features of the watch, which isn't surprising but should be kept in mind.

------Otherwise, there is a sufficient night-glow material on the 1-12 hour indicators, hour, minute, second, and tide hand that allow time easy night time readings. It seems to lose it's bright glow capability rather quickly, however it maintains a dim glow that's still easily legible after many hours in the dark.

COMPASS - Impressively accurate and offers declination input corrections. The compass is spot on with my hand-held compass and maps of my area. My local area requires a declination correction of 5 degrees West. While in the compass mode you can pull out the crown and input declination corrections as needed by turning the crown up or down to set the degrees of correction. Some maps provide declination data, however most civilian maps don't offer this information from my experience. If you need help, you can easily find your area's declination correction from NOAA's website here: https://www.ngdc.noaa.gov/geomag/declination.shtml

BAROMETRIC PRESSURE (inHg or hPa) - The watch continuously monitors barometric pressure with a 20hr history regardless of what mode you're in. The manual suggests a barometer correction is most likely not needed to be changed from the default settings and recommends to avoid making any corrections. However, I believe this is incorrect in my opinion. All barometers most likely need an accuracy correction specific to your location from another independent barometer to receive the most accurate results. Ironically, the manual conflicts itself as it then goes on to state the same. My first reading was 0.5 inHg offset with local readings upon unboxing. While this was still rather accurate, I adjusted to match my local city barometer readings. Once I matched with local readings, it has remained constant with local readings within 0.00inHg-0.05inHg. Very impressive!

--- In addition, while in time keeping mode if you hold down "C" for 3 seconds you'll activate the Barometer mini dial gauge which will provide you with immediate updates in slight or rapid changes in the increase or decrease in Barometric pressure. While in this mode there are 4 different sounding alarms for each state (which the manual does not cover): slight increasing, rapid increasing, slight decreasing, rapid decreasing. The most notable alarm is the rapid decreasing (ie. bad weather), it's alarm tone is similar to an emergency vehicle (high tone, low tone, high tone, low town). Note: while you've engaged this mode, you are "actively" monitoring Barometric pressure which means the watch will not automatically go into power saving mode (if you have it active) and will not receive a radio-signal to update the time. You can only be in this "active" barometric mode for 24hours, after that the watch will automatically revert back time-keeping mode and the mini-dial will revert to tide data.

---Barometric Pressure Differential - While in Barometer mode you can set to measure changes in barometric pressure by pressing C. The second hand will then point to the change in pressure by pointing to one of the numerical values on the outer external bezel ring that reads 1-10 and + OVER and 1-10 and -UNDER. Every 1 = 1hPa or .03inHg. +OVER reading means the differential change is more than 10hPa/0.3inHg and -UNDER means the reading is more than -10hpa/-0.3inHG.

ALTIMETER (ft or meters) - The elevation where I live is approx. 580ft and I got a reading of 595ft out of the box with no adjustment. This was accurate to my location and required no adjustment. I live in a 2 story house and while measuring altitude, the sensor detects when I go downstairs or upstairs changing from 565ft (downstairs) to 585ft (upstairs), which was very accurate. It should be noted the Altimeter and Barometer sensors are one in the same. If the barometric pressure changes, so will the altitude. If your altitude changes, so will the barometric reading. Thus, if you intend on using the Altimeter frequently, you will need to calibrate it any time you use it if it's not currently calibrated to your location and current weather conditions. This is also confirmed in the manual.

---Altitude Differential - While measuring altitude you can set the altitude differential to provide readings to read change in altitude in by holding down "C" for approx. 3 seconds. You can change the increments by pulling out the crown and selecting 100m or 100m increments. While measuring the differential, the second hand will point to the outer external bezel ring that reads 1-10 and + OVER and 1-10 and -UNDER. Every 1 = 10m/100m or 32.8ft/328ft (depending on units of measurement you've selected and incremental settings selected). +OVER means the reading is more than 100m/1000m or 328ft/3280ft and -UNDER means the reading is more than -100m/-1000m or -328ft/-3280ft.

------The differential can be reset at any time by holding "C" for two seconds. In addition, you can have the watch log this data over 1hour or 12 hours by entering in Altimeter mode, pulling the crown out, pressing B to navigate to "INT" mode and selecting 005 (1hr) or 1200 (12hrs).

THERMOMETER - This is the sensor that seems to get the most hate due to it having to be off your wrist for 20-30mins to get an accurate reading. That said, there's no logical way any watch or thermometer, no matter how accurate the sensor, can accurately detect local air temperatures while on your wrist or touching your skin due to your residual body temperature influencing the readings. However with another local thermometer, time, and a little bit of math, it's completely possible to gather accurate readings indoors with the watch alone once you've done a little bit of testing. I keep multiple temperature gauges in my house and have one at my work. My computer room averages 10 degrees hotter than the rest of the house. After taking a few readings while staying in various rooms around the house, I noticed a consistent correlation in difference of degrees between the watch and local room temp. readings. My watch consistently read 14 degrees warmer than local room temps, regardless of which room I was in. I conducted the same testing at work indoors and concluded the same. So to get an accurate reading indoors, I just subtract 14 degrees while indoors (yours may vary). Although, outdoors is a different story; it's the least accurate when doing this math while on your wrist. The temperature reading variance is much more severe outdoors depending on the conditions. For example, the watch read 88 degrees while at work indoors (which was accurate as after subtracting 14 it was 74 degrees which matched my other thermometer), however upon going outside where the temperature was 54 degrees, the reading actually increased to 92. This was due to the sun shining on the watch and heating up the components. Thus, I was looking at a 38 degree difference. I honestly don't think there's a way to accurately detect outdoor temperature due to there being too many variables that come into play that's not present indoors (direct sunlight, rain, shade, humidity, etc.)

MOON AGE - The watch will provide you the current moon age in a numerical value only (no graphics). If you're not sure how moon age works, this information is completely useless. However, if you understand this numerical value, it provides you with a gold mine of information! Moon age alone can provide you with accurate estimates and predictions of illumination data down to the hour, day, weeks, and even months, and; provide moon rise/moon set times. For example, the below data should give you an indication of how useful moon age can be and shows how you can predict illumination data and moon rise/set times based on moon age alone.

---Moon Age (first day of each cycle listed below is the current moon):

0.0-7/8 days (New Moon) *Illumination - 0% -> 50%
7/8-14/15 days (First Quarter) *Illumination - 50% -> 100%
14/15-22/23 days (Full Moon) *Illumination - 100% -> 50%
22/23-29 days (Last Quarter) *Illumination - 50% -> 0%

New Moon - Rise 6am / Mid 12pm / Set 6pm ->
First Quarter - Rise 12pm / Mid 6pm / Set 12am ->
Full Moon - Rise 6pm / Mid 12am / Set 6am ->
Last Quarter - Rise 12am / Mid 6am / 12pm

---Thus, if the moon age reads 7 days, you know that your entering the First Quarter and should have a half-moon (approx. 50% illumination) with a rise of 12pm, mid-point of 6pm, and set at 12am. Then, for any additional days after that you just add 1hr to the rise and set (add 2 hrs for the rise, if it's the next day). So for example, assuming full moon is age 14 and it's day 15 on your watch, then the moon rise will be 7pm and set 7am the next day - give or take 1mins-30mins.

------It's important to keep in mind however, that the moon's orbit is not constant. Hence the reason for the variation above of "/" and why a full moon may occur on age 13, 14, or even 15. But it usually doesn't exceed 2 day variances or so. So while you may not be getting absolute perfect moon data. It's more than enough to get an accurate reading of what you've got to work with, with only a 2 day or so error for the moon cycle and a 1 hour error rate for the moon rise/set rate throughout the year.

ALARMS: 5 alarms, each independent and capable of being turned on/off

COUNTDOWN TIMER: 1 hr count down timer. Can be set to any measurement one hour or less.

STOPWATCH: 24 hr max.

TIDE READINGS: I honestly wish I could provide a better review on the accuracy of this, but I can't. I live too far from the East coast to tell you if it's accurate. But from other East Coast reviews I've seen, it's reported that it's accurate. In addition, I've compared it to east coast tide reports online daily, and so far it's been very accurate and consistent with local online tide reports. (Ex. It's currently 9:27pm an I have a reading of low tide on my watch right now and current tide reports for Savannah, GA reads low tide at 9:29pm.). I can't say the same for the West Coast though. The few reviews I've seen suggest west coast reviews are not quite as accurate. But it should be noted, regardless of location, it will never be 100% accurate, there are other factors that affect tides. The good news is, you can manually adjust the tide data information on the watch if this is important to you.

POWER SAVE MODE ON/OFF: When the Power Saving mode is ON and after 60-70mins in the dark (display sleep), the digital display goes blank and the second hand stops. After 6-7 days in the dark, the digital display goes blanks, all hands stop at 12 o'clock and all functions are disabled (function sleep). While the watch is in either Power Saving mode, it will not receive a radio atomic correction. However, I've found it best to keep the power saving mode on as the watch was designed smart enough to still receive it's signal daily, either right before it goes into display sleep or right after. The watch will not enter a sleep state during the hours of 6am or 9:59pm. Again, it will not go into a sleep state AT ALL if light is detected.

BATTERY/SOLAR INFORMATION: Anytime while navigating through the watch and going back to timekeeping mode or back to your default digital display reading the watch will indicate it's current battery level by briefly displaying "L/M/H TIME". L = Low battery, M = Medium, H = near or full charged. When I first received the watch it was on medium battery levels and since then likes to hover around medium to high. The solar ability is extremely efficient! After days of no or little sun, just a few hours in the sun will charge it to "H". I use all features on this watch daily and frequently throughout the day. Even then, I've never ran it to a low battery. The manual lists specifics of charge times to get from L->M->H and expected battery life in each value with theoretical no additional solar power. Without listing each, I'm very impressed with it's usage and capability on the battery. From "H" levels you can use every function daily for approx. 6-7 months with no sun-light until it goes dead.

-----------CONS (ORDERED WITH 1 BEING THE WORST):------------------

1. Back-light timing - For some reason Casio has programmed their back lighting to immediately turn off if another button is pressed. This can make visual navigation through the watch's settings and features extremely aggravating and difficult at night! If you're trying to navigate through the watch's features/settings, you're forced to press the back-light button, press another button (then the light immediately turns off regardless of timing settings or how long the light's been on), then you have to press the back-light again; and repeat until you've done what you got to do. I've noticed most Casio/G-shock watches do this and it baffles me. Why not have the light run through it's 1 or 3 second timer so you can navigate through the watch's functions? Better yet, have the light remain on while pressing buttons and navigating through the features. Perhaps this is a battery saving feature? Maybe, although there are many other watches on the market do not turn off when another button is pressed and it seems like it's such a simple concept; so I'm not sure why Casio has never addressed this issue or fixed this problem.

*Auto-Back light - While this is a really nice feature, I find it to erroneously active the light a bit too frequently while working on the computer, driving, or placing my hands in my lap while sitting down in low light. However, it does not activate at all while you're in lighted conditions as it detects it's not needed (very nice Casio!). Other than that, this is no biggie, you can turn the auto-feature off if it's not working for you.

2. Alarm Settings: Setting an alarm can take a VERY long time to navigate to the time you need set. Setting a numerical time value on the digital display is done by rotating the crown or knob in either direction to reach your desired time. While you can scroll the knob fast 3 times and it will automatically travel at HS1 (High-speed autonomous movement) ahead or backwards so you don't have to keep scrolling the crown, there's nothing high-speed about it. For example, if you use the 24hr format and need to set an alarm for 1800 hours, it starts at 0000 and moves forward at 10 digital mins. per 1 second. Thus, it will take 60 seconds to reach 1800 hours from 0000. Yes I know, you could go backwards and only have to travel 6 hours, but it's just an example. This just feels odd to have to wait this long and makes it near impossible to set an alarm on the fly.

3. Alarm Audio Tones: The alarm audio levels and duration is sub-par in my opinion. The alarm decibel level is lower than what I'd like it to be and only activates for 10 seconds. I'd prefer to see a bit of a louder alarm that's capable of adjusting the duration of the alarm up to a minimum of 60 seconds...but that's just me.

4. Countdown timer: 1 hour. That's all you get. If you need a timer for anything more than 1 hour you're SOL. Why Casio?! I own a $30 Casio that offers a 24 hour countdown timer, so I'm not sure who decided this feature was not needed. But I'm really surprised this is the maximum limit you get on this watch.

5. No Sunrise/Sunset data: My assumption is that adding in longitude/latitude software would've required larger hardware which would make the watch too big...at least that's what I hope Casio's reasoning is behind not adding in this feature. Sunrise/sunset data is something I would've loved to see included in this watch and would've probably made it the most versatile non-smart/solar powered watch on the market. This would've made it into the "perfection" lists of outdoor watches in my opinion, so it's sad to not see it on this watch.

6. Negative Display - The negative display can be a bit difficult to take a quick reading in lighted and mainly sunny conditions, especially if held at a certain angles. However, when used in low-light or no-light conditions or utilizing the back light, this is no issue. Otherwise, once angled correctly the negative display is no issue and easily legible in all lighted conditions.

7. Crown/Scroll Wheel Weakness - this, perhaps, may be the largest con or the least con, depending on usage and who you ask - hence why I put it last. While this is a 20BAR/200 meter water resistant, shock resident G-shock - known for their durability and I'm sure it can take quite a beating. It's Achilles heel appears to be the Crown/scroll wheel. While not in use and stowed away in the lock position, you get all the protection stated above. However, there's indication that if the crown is in it's outer or unlocked position, it's prone to receive water damage or exposure to other elements that may destroy your $400 watch. The manual also states if the crown is left in the unlocked position, you'll risk damage to the watch - but doesn't go into detail how. My assumption is by pulling out the crown, you negate vital seals to keep the internals safe and dry. I don't know the extent of it's vulnerability while the crown is unlocked, and I've not seen any forums or other users reporting damage in this fashion; but the fact that the manual clearly states the risks makes me rather cautious. All of that said...just keep that in mind you'll need to double and triple check the crown is pushed in and locked after use and just assume the worse will happen if you don't.

*Note - Thankfully, the ABC-T functions and most of the functionalities of the watch does not require you to pull out the crown as most features are accessible through the button presses alone. So that's a plus.

OVERALL AND SUMMARY:

Taking everything into consideration, this is by far the most appealing, best looking, and functional ABC watch I've ever had the pleasure of owning. The build quality is superb and feels very high-quality; and it's resistant specifications to the elements are exceptional.

All of it's sensors are highly accurate and it's features and navigation through said features are a breeze. The button actions and mode selections make it very easy to get to where you want to go or get from where you are to another location within the watch quickly.

Most reviews I've seen rated the watch's learning curve high, however I'd put it closer to medium if you have even a basic knowledge of digital watches and have elementary knowledge on the functions of compass, barometer, altimeter, and temperature readings. If you have never owned a digital watch and/or are not computer or tech savvy, than the learning curve may be a bit higher, but still easily learned within a few days.

While I would like to see the Cons I mentioned addressed and corrected by Casio, they are in no way a deal breaker for me. It's just when I pay $400 for a watch, the nit-picky things tend to irk me as you expect near perfection. Furthermore, they are small details that should not cause high cost changes on Casio's end to address and should not affect the price of the watch for the consumer.

My conclusion: it's the perfect watch for casual wear, looks fantastic, yet has all of the outdoor features you need and deserves a 5 star rating and I don't feel the cons deserve a deduction in a star due to the overall quality of this watch.

If I run into any issues in any way or if I find I need any corrections/updates to my review, I will be back to update my review.

-------UPDATE 3 STARS 01/12---------
We had abnormally low temperatures for our area in the 20's last week. I noticed when I would go outdoors condensation built up in center of the glass within the watch (pic added). I tested this thoroughly and this only occurs when the watch is on your wrist. If you just place the watch outside, it doesn't do this. It seems to react to rapid changes in temperatures from warm indoors to below freezing/high-moisture environments while on your wrist. It seems your body temperature in combination with extreme cold climates causes the condensation. I was able to recreate it by placing an ice cube on the watch glass while indoors.

My suspicion goes back to the Achilles heel of the crown. When you pull out that crown it allows any moisture in the air to enter the watch and remain there - but this is just a guess. This causes the watch to react with condensation when entering cold-wet climates in combination with your warm wrist. However, I have noticed after an hour or so remaining outdoors it clears up. It also clears up quickly once you re-enter a warmer climate. So far, it has never left a spec of water markings within the watch glass

Days of research online suggests this is nothing to worry about. So long as the condensation clears quickly while transitioning from outdoors to indoors, you do not have a sealant problem. But if it takes a while to clear up, it may be a sealant issue. This seems to be a common "feature" of G-shocks or other brand watches with a Crown feature and I was unable to find any reports of damage to a G-shock when this occurs. I even found some G-shock manuals stating this is normal. At the moment from everything I've read, it doesn't mean the watch is no longer water proof up to it's reported capability. Thus, for now, I'm not too worried about it.

However, that said, even though manuals and other users have claimed this to be normal for the most part. The bottom-line is water and electronics don't mix. In addition, I've owned $20 digital watches that don't do this; but then again, they don't have a crown. All-in-all it sure is crappy owning a $400 watch and having condensation appear in cold wet climates. It affects your ability to read the watch and gives the appearance that it's waterproof capability is compromised. I was also not able to find in this particular manual describing if condensation was normal or not.

Thus, I'm lowering to 3 stars because this should not occur in my honest opinion...period. Regardless whether or not it's safe or nothing to worry about, it's an expensive watch. Luckily, we rarely experience temperatures below freezing, but this may not be the same for other potential buyers, so this may be a deal breaker. I still love this watch and it still dang near deserves a 5 star rating in my book for it's looks, features, functionality, in addition to my climate. But this may not hold true for other potential buyers who live in colder climates, so my updated review reflect
